title: Neoporteria odieri
intro: Neoporteria odieri, also known as Chuquiraga, is a type of flowering plant native to the Andes mountains in South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Neoporteria odieri has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antidiabetic, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for managing conditions such as arthritis and diabetes. The plant contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neoporteria odieri is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into ointments and creams for topical application.
taxonomy: Neoporteria odieri is classified within the family Cactaceae, a large and diverse family of cacti that includes species found in the Americas. The genus Neoporteria is native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountain range. Neoporteria odieri is part of the subfamily Cactoideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within the Cactaceae family. This classification places Neoporteria odieri among other species of cacti that share similar characteristics and growth habits. Further classification is possible at the species level, but more specific information may be required to determine the exact classification within the species.
